purpose: Operator-pickable target feature for TASK-HMIG-010's end-to-end LangGraph validation run. Mirrors the canary-set.json pattern from TASK-HMIG-009: candidates with rationale, fit scoring against AC-001, and an operator_pick slot that the operator fills in BEFORE running guardkit autobuild feature.

ac_001_selection_criteria.note_on_criteria_strictness: These criteria together are tight. As of 2026-06-04 no single backlog feature satisfies all five (the BDD-gated + ≤8h pair is the hardest constraint — only beads-integration has a BDD-gated task and it's a ~30-40h feature). Operator may either pick a sub-slice of a larger feature, relax one criterion with rationale, or pick the closest fit and document the gap. The relaxation rationale should be recorded in operator_pick.criteria_deviation.

fit_against_ac_001.state_bridge_tasks: TBD — needs manual scan; CLI integration tasks (BI-006, BI-007) likely touch state_bridge transitions
fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Strong on BDD coverage + structure (4 waves, 11 tasks); fails on total effort

candidates.rationale: The only backlog feature with a BDD-gated task (TASK-BI-009 has bdd_scenarios: [...]). 4-wave structure exercises the full WorktreeManager + feature_complete merge paths. Real feature delivery value (backend abstraction for task storage — Markdown vs Beads-Beanstalkd). Strong candidate IF sliced.

slice_recommendation.rationale: BI-001 (interface) + BI-002 (markdown backend) + BI-004 (registry) form a self-contained Wave 1+2 slice that delivers a working TaskBackend abstraction without committing to the beads-beanstalkd implementation. Adding BI-009 (unified metadata schema, BDD-gated) gives the BDD coverage AC-001 requires. Estimated 4 tasks × ~2h orchestrator-time = ~8h. Fits the ≤8h bound exactly.
slice_recommendation.caveat: The slice leaves BI-003 (beads-beanstalkd backend) + BI-005..BI-008 + BI-010/011 in backlog. That's acceptable; they can be picked up post-cutover by /feature-build or further /task-work invocations.

fit_against_ac_001.state_bridge_tasks: Likely — TASK-FIX-GD02 (shared-worktree git detection baseline) touches the WorktreeManager + state_bridge boundary
fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Fits structure + effort + state-bridge; FAILS BDD criterion

candidates.rationale: Smallest backlog feature that cleanly fits the structure-and-effort half of AC-001. 3 tasks, 2 waves, ~6-8h. The state-bridge boundary is exercised (GD02 specifically). Delivers real fixes to the autobuild observability surface that the operator presumably wants anyway. Operator must relax the BDD criterion to pick this — the relaxation rationale would be: 'BDD-plugin contract C1 was already validated by 009A's 12-run batch under qwen36-workhorse; re-exercising it under feature-level orchestration adds little marginal coverage beyond the WorktreeManager + feature_complete paths.'

fit_against_ac_001.state_bridge_tasks: Unlikely — these are pure CLI + Graphiti-integration tasks
fit_against_ac_001.estimated_orchestrator_hours: 2-4 (complexity 1+3+7, but the 7-complexity one is review_complete so effectively 1+3=4)
fit_against_ac_001.verdict: Fits structure; UNDERSHOOTS effort (too small to exercise feature-build at load); FAILS BDD + state-bridge

candidates.rationale: Listed for completeness as the smallest 3-task / 2-wave backlog candidate. Probably too lightweight to meaningfully validate the WorktreeManager + feature_complete + Coach-gating-across-multiple-tasks paths the task is designed to exercise. Not recommended unless operator wants a fast, low-cost smoke and is willing to accept that 010 will not stress-test the orchestration surface.

execution_plan_once_picked.step_1_baseline: Confirm the picked tasks are clean in backlog (not in_progress, not blocked). If a slice is being used, file a placeholder note in the feature folder's README documenting which tasks are in/out of the 010 slice so subsequent /feature-build runs don't re-pick them.
execution_plan_once_picked.step_3_run: GUARDKIT_HARNESS=langgraph OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://promaxgb10-41b1:9000/v1 OPENAI_API_KEY=llama-swap-local-key guardkit autobuild feature {feature_id} --model qwen36-workhorse 2>&1 | tee .guardkit/autobuild/TASK-REV-HMIG-feature-run/run-1-stdout.log
execution_plan_once_picked.step_4_per_task_record: After each task completes (success or failure), append a record to TASK-REV-HMIG-feature-results.json matching the AC-003 schema. The runner does not yet auto-emit this — manual append is the current shape (mirrors 009A's runner-parser gap; see TASK-FIX-CANARY-PARSER).
execution_plan_once_picked.step_5_resume_on_failure: Any first-pass failure → guardkit autobuild feature {feature_id} --resume. Record resume outcome and first-pass-fail reason in the same per-task record.
execution_plan_once_picked.step_6_merge: On all-pass: guardkit autobuild complete {feature_id}. Confirm no merge conflicts, no broken downstream consumers (AC-007).
execution_plan_once_picked.step_7_analysis: Append narrative to docs/state/TASK-REV-HMIG/feature-run-analysis.md per AC-009. Compare first-pass-success rate to 009A's 67% LangGraph baseline (AC-004). Compute falsifier verdict per AC-008.

model_choice.rationale: Same model as 009A's validated batch. No re-validation needed; the 009A 12-run batch proved this substrate satisfies the Player+Coach contract end-to-end under both wire formats.

notes_for_operator: AC-001 selection is the load-bearing decision in this task. The /task-work scaffold has presented 3 candidates with explicit fit-against-AC-001 scoring; pick one and fill the operator_pick block.
The beads-integration sliced option is the only candidate that satisfies all 5 criteria simultaneously, at the cost of committing to deliver a real (large) feature in slices over time.
The autobuild-observability-fixes option is the cleanest fit if you're willing to relax the BDD criterion. Recommended relaxation rationale provided above.
Falsifier verdict (AC-008): ≥80% first-pass-success AND no non-recoverable failure → proceed to Wave 4 cutover (TASK-HMIG-011). Anything else → halt cutover, escalate.
Schedule: spec says D-9 → D-7 so results inform the D-7 cutover-flip decision. Deadline is 2026-06-15 so D-7 ≈ 2026-06-08; budget ~2-3 days for the run + analysis.
